reddit.copy:
I've spent countless hours writing cold emails that actually convert, and I've learned that it's an art that requires a mix of creativity, personalization, and persistence. Through trial and error, I've managed to craft emails that have helped me land meetings with potential clients and grow my business, but it's not always easy and I've had my fair share of rejections.

I've tried using existing tools like email templates and automated writing software, but they often fell short in terms of personalization and tone. These tools would spit out generic emails that lacked the human touch, and I found myself spending more time editing and customizing them than if I were to write the emails from scratch. I also tried hiring freelance writers, but that was expensive and didn't always yield the results I was looking for. Despite my best efforts, I just couldn't seem to find a solution that worked for me.

I decided to take matters into my own hands and built a tool that I call Cold Email Writer. It's a simple web app that helps me generate personalized cold emails based on a few inputs like the company name, job title, and industry. I built it using a combination of natural language processing and machine learning algorithms, and it's been a game changer for my business. One thing that still doesn't work perfectly is the tone analysis feature, but I'm working on it. Built this at https://cold-email-writer-tool-2yg128czk-sweths-projects-68683994.vercel.app — would love feedback from this community
reddit.instructions: Go to r/marketing, click New Post, paste copy, post

reddit.copy:
I've always struggled to write follow-up emails quickly, often spending way too much time crafting the perfect message. I've found myself procrastinating on sending follow-ups, which has definitely hurt my chances of getting responses and closing deals.

I've tried using existing tools like email templates and automation software, but they always seemed to fall short - either they were too rigid and didn't allow for enough customization, or they were so complex that I ended up spending even more time trying to set them up. I've also attempted to create my own templates, but I'd always end up tweaking them for too long, trying to get the tone just right. None of these solutions really stuck, and I was back to square one, typing out follow-ups from scratch every time.

So, I decided to build my own tool to help with this - FollowUp Writer. I cobbled it together using some spare time and a lot of trial and error, and it's been a game-changer for me. The tool still isn't perfect - the tone suggestions can be a bit off sometimes - but it's saved me a ton of time and helped me stay on top of my follow-ups. Built this at https://followup-writer-tool-9dm58rpie-sweths-projects-68683994.vercel.app — would love feedback from this community

reddit.copy:
I've always struggled to come up with compelling subject lines for my emails, but over the past few months, I've noticed a significant improvement in my writing. I've been experimenting with different techniques and tools, and it's made a huge difference in my email open rates.

I tried using existing tools like email subject line generators and analyzers, but they often produced cheesy or overly promotional results that didn't quite fit my tone. I also attempted to use machine learning models to generate subject lines, but they required a lot of data and computational power, making them impractical for my needs. These solutions were either too simplistic or too complex, and I found myself spending more time tweaking their output than actually writing.

I built SubjectLine Pro as a solution to my own problems, using a combination of natural language processing and simple algorithms to suggest subject lines based on the content of my email. It's not perfect - sometimes it still suggests subject lines that are a bit too long - but it's been a game changer for me. Built this at https://email-subject-writer-qz837fa8s-sweths-projects-68683994.vercel.app — would love feedback from this community
